title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Quin and Inspector Richard Quin visit Sheriff Redden in Weldon, West Virginia, to investigate the death of Andrew Van. Sheriff Redden explains that on Christmas morning, a farmer named Mike O'Keefe and a man known as Old Pete discovered Van's body hanging from a signpost at a crossroads. The Sheriff, along with Mayor Matt Hollis and four others, inspected and identified the body.

The investigation revealed that Van's house showed signs of a violent struggle, featuring bloodied furniture and a large "T" painted in blood on the door. Van's servant, Clark, has disappeared, and an axe belonging to Clark—which bore no fingerprints—was found at the scene. Because Van's head was missing, the Sheriff speculated that the killer took it as a souvenir. The body was sent to the coroner in Wilton County, and both the state police and the Hanover County district attorney have become involved.

Following the meeting, Ellery and the Inspector depart by train, though Ellery returns to Weldon on Tuesday. He attempts to obtain information from District Attorney Crummett, but the ambitious official refuses to see him. As Ellery paces the streets, he observes a town gripped by fear and suppressed excitement; while the townspeople celebrate the holidays, the men discuss potential lynching, though no suspect has been identified.
